items.title: Trump says Iran violated ceasefire with drone strike on cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: President Trump on Friday accused Iran of violating the ceasefire with the U.S. after Iranian drones attacked commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.
items.content: President Trump on Friday accused Iran of violating the ceasefire with the U.S. after Iranian drones attacked commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Nikole Killion has more.

items.title: Trump blames Iran for drone strike on cargo ship in strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: US president calls it ‘foolish violation’ of ceasefire agreement after drone damaged ship’s upper deckUS politics live – latest updatesDonald Trump on Friday blamed Iran for carrying out a drone strike on a cargo ship in the strait of Hormuz, calling it a “foolish violation” of the ceasefire agreement with the US.One drone damaged the upper deck of the ship but the ship was able to proceed, the US president said. The US shot down three other drones aimed at the ship, he said. Continue reading...

The US shot down three other drones aimed at the ship, he said. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, along with Israel and Lebanon’s ambassadors, announced a framework agreement Friday that was described as a first step toward peace following months of conflict between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah
items.content: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, along with Israel and Lebanon’s ambassadors, announced a framework agreement Friday that was described as a first step toward peace following months of conflict between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah

items.content: President Trump accused Iran of a "foolish violation" of the ceasefire with the U.S. after an Iranian drone struck a cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Ed O'Keefe has more.

items.title: Middle East war: Lebanon, Israel and US sign framework agreement in Washington
items.standfirst: ⁠US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Friday a framework agreement between ‌Israel ⁠and Lebanon ‌following talks held ⁠in Washington. Nada Hamadeh, ambassador of Lebanon to the United States described the framework as "a first step on the road of restoring Lebanese sovereignty and territorial integrity".
items.content: ⁠US Secretary of State Marco Rubio announced Friday a framework agreement between ‌Israel ⁠and Lebanon ‌following talks held ⁠in Washington. Nada Hamadeh, ambassador of Lebanon to the United States described the framework as "a first step on the road of restoring Lebanese sovereignty and territorial integrity".

clusters.title: John Bolton faces prison time after pleading guilty in classified documents case

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World, Financial Times and others

items.title: John Bolton faces prison time after pleading guilty in classified documents case
items.standfirst: Former National Security adviser John Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining classified information and faces up to five years in prison and a fine. CBS News' Jake Rosen has the latest.
items.content: Former National Security adviser John Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining classified information and faces up to five years in prison and a fine. CBS News' Jake Rosen has the latest.

items.title: Ex-Trump adviser John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified documents
items.standfirst: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.
items.content: Bolton faces a prison sentence of up to five years and has agreed to pay $2.25m in fine, prosecutors say.

items.title: John Bolton pleads guilty to retaining classified information
items.standfirst: Bolton agreed to pay a $2.25 million fine, pleading guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information.
items.content: Bolton agreed to pay a $2.25 million fine, pleading guilty to one count of retaining classified national security information.

items.title: John Bolton pleads guilty to mishandling classified material

items.content: Plea by former US national security adviser marks first victory in Donald Trump’s legal campaign against his political foes

items.title: Former Trump national security adviser Bolton pleads guilty in classified files case
items.standfirst: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to US President Donald Trump who has since become one of his most vocal critics, pleaded guilty Friday to withholding classified documents related to national defence.
items.content: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to US President Donald Trump who has since become one of his most vocal critics, pleaded guilty Friday to withholding classified documents related to national defence.

items.title: John Bolton, former Trump national security adviser, pleads guilty in classified documents case
items.standfirst: Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."
items.content: Bolton pleaded guilty to one count of retaining national defense information while he was a national security adviser during President Trump's first term, saying: "And I am sorry for it."(Image credit: Alex Kent)

clusters.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
clusters.neutral_headline: Rescuers race to find survivors of Venezuelan earthquakes
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera, NPR News and others

items.title: Race to find survivors as Venezuela's earthquake death toll soars
items.standfirst: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.
items.content: The twin earthquakes that hit Venezuela killed more than 900 people, and that toll is likely to keep rising as frantic rescue and recovery operations ramp up.

items.title: Rescuers race to find survivors of Venezuelan earthquakes
items.standfirst: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to find earthquake survivors as the critical 72-hour "golden window" nears.
items.content: Rescue teams in Venezuela are racing to find earthquake survivors as the critical 72-hour "golden window" nears.

items.title: Rescuers scramble to find Venezuela earthquake survivors. And, SCOTUS rules on asylum
items.standfirst: Rescuers are searching for thousands of people missing after two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. And, the Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border.
items.content: Rescuers are searching for thousands of people missing after two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday. And, the Supreme Court says the U.S. can turn away asylum seekers at the border.(Image credit: Jesus Vargas)

items.title: Venezuelans hurry through quake rubble in search for thousands of survivors
items.standfirst: Massive search and rescue operations are underway in Venezuela on Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 589 people.
items.content: Massive search and rescue operations are underway in Venezuela on Friday as civilians and emergency workers rushed to find those trapped beneath the rubble more than a day after devastating twin earthquakes killed over 589 people.

items.title: Two Massive Earthquakes Struck Venezuela. Thousands Are Feared Dead
items.standfirst: The country’s interim leader declared a state of emergency on Wednesday following shocks measuring 7.5 magnitude after two quakes hit in less than a minute.
items.content: The country’s interim leader declared a state of emergency on Wednesday following shocks measuring 7.5 magnitude after two quakes hit in less than a minute.

items.title: See the aftermath of the Venezuela earthquakes
items.standfirst: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela on Wednesday, the first measuring a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5. The country's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, declared a state of emergency. Videos show the heavy damage in the capital of Caracas as rescue crews look for survivors.
items.content: Two earthquakes hit Venezuela on Wednesday, the first measuring a magnitude of 7.2 and the second 7.5. The country's acting President, Delcy Rodriguez, declared a state of emergency. Videos show the heavy damage in the capital of Caracas as rescue crews look for survivors.

items.title: Trump threatens 100% tariffs on Europe if countries impose tax on US digital services
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump threatened Friday to impose 100 percent tariffs on European countries that implement a digital services tax, even if it means canceling existing trade agreements.
items.content: US President Donald Trump threatened Friday to impose 100 percent tariffs on European countries that implement a digital services tax, even if it means canceling existing trade agreements.

items.title: Trump warns of 100% tariff on countries implementing digital services tax
items.standfirst: US president says levy would supersede any trade deals Washington has with other capitals
items.content: US president says levy would supersede any trade deals Washington has with other capitals

items.title: Trump Vows 100% Tariff on Europe Over Digital Services Taxes
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump threatened to impose 100% tariffs on goods imported from countries that impose digital services taxes, escalating pressure on European nations that just ratified a trade pact with the US.
items.content: President Donald Trump threatened to impose 100% tariffs on goods imported from countries that impose digital services taxes, escalating pressure on European nations that just ratified a trade pact with the US.

clusters.title: Venezuelans search for missing as earthquake death toll climbs
clusters.neutral_headline: Venezuelans search for missing as earthquake death toll climbs
bullet_summary: Satellite images taken in La Guaira State, the area hardest hit by the double earthquake that struck Venezuela on Wednesday, June 24, have revealed the scale of the devastation in the country
Reported by France24, Wired, NBC World

items.title: Venezuelans search for missing as earthquake death toll climbs
items.standfirst: The death toll from the twin earthquakes in Venezuela continued to rise on Friday, with at least 920 dead and more than 51,000 missing, as distress grew among Venezuelans. The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes that struck late Wednesday left a landscape of devastation, with countless collapsed buildings, particularly in La Guaira, a coastal town near Caracas, where residents are criticising the government's lack of action in rescue operations.
items.content: The death toll from the twin earthquakes in Venezuela continued to rise on Friday, with at least 920 dead and more than 51,000 missing, as distress grew among Venezuelans. The 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude quakes that struck late Wednesday left a landscape of devastation, with countless collapsed buildings, particularly in La Guaira, a coastal town near Caracas, where residents are criticising the government's lack of action in rescue operations.

items.title: Venezuela earthquakes: Before and after images reveal scale of devastation
items.standfirst: Satellite images taken in La Guaira State, the area hardest hit by the double earthquake that struck Venezuela on Wednesday, June 24, have revealed the scale of the devastation in the country. As the search for survivors goes on the death toll from the quakes continues to rise, with at least 589 people killed and nearly 3,000 injured.
items.content: Satellite images taken in La Guaira State, the area hardest hit by the double earthquake that struck Venezuela on Wednesday, June 24, have revealed the scale of the devastation in the country. As the search for survivors goes on the death toll from the quakes continues to rise, with at least 589 people killed and nearly 3,000 injured.

items.title: Why Venezuela’s ‘doublet’ earthquakes were so devastating
items.standfirst: The tremors that hit Venezuela on Wednesday evening have killed nearly 600 people and injured thousands. The disaster consisted of a rare phenomenon called a “doublet”, meaning a quick succession of major “twin” earthquakes.
items.content: The tremors that hit Venezuela on Wednesday evening have killed nearly 600 people and injured thousands. The disaster consisted of a rare phenomenon called a “doublet”, meaning a quick succession of major “twin” earthquakes.

items.title: Venezuela’s Powerful Earthquakes Were a Rare ‘Seismic Doublet’
items.standfirst: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.
items.content: The country was hit hard by a pair of quakes that happened in quick succession and were likely driven by stress being transferred from one part of the fault that runs through the country to another.

items.title: What to know about ‘doublet’ earthquakes like the destructive pair that hit Venezuela
items.standfirst: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered a doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that most likely led to more destruction.
items.content: Venezuela’s pair of earthquakes is considered a doublet sequence. It’s not rare to have two quakes in short succession, but in this case, that most likely led to more destruction.

clusters.title: Advocates warn of wide-ranging implications of US Supreme Court TPS ruling
clusters.neutral_headline: Advocates warn of wide-ranging implications of US Supreme Court TPS ruling
bullet_summary: Reported by Al Jazeera, France24, BBC World

items.title: Advocates warn of wide-ranging implications of US Supreme Court TPS ruling
items.standfirst: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haiti and Syria, threatening family separation say advocates.
items.content: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haiti and Syria, threatening family separation say advocates.

items.title: Over a million immigrants risk deportation from US after landmark decision
items.standfirst: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump Administration another win on its policies aggressively cracking down on immigration and allowing mass deportations. In the conservative majority's 6-3 decision -the justices ruled that Washington can lift the temporary protected status of hundreds of thousands of Haitian and Syrian migrants in the United States. That decision paves the way for those TPS holders to be sent back to their home countries.
items.content: The US Supreme Court has handed the Trump Administration another win on its policies aggressively cracking down on immigration and allowing mass deportations. In the conservative majority's 6-3 decision -the justices ruled that Washington can lift the temporary protected status of hundreds of thousands of Haitian and Syrian migrants in the United States. That decision paves the way for those TPS holders to be sent back to their home countries.

items.title: In win for Trump, US Supreme Court paves way for mass deportation of Haitians and Syrians
items.standfirst: The US Supreme Court has backed a Trump administration move to strip deportation protections from some 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians living in the United States. The decision effectively stops migrants from being able to make the asylum claim. One of three dissident Justices criticised the role race played in the decision to strip Haitians of their temporary protected status. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert tells us why this decision is a turning point for immigration in the US.
items.content: The US Supreme Court has backed a Trump administration move to strip deportation protections from some 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians living in the United States. The decision effectively stops migrants from being able to make the asylum claim. One of three dissident Justices criticised the role race played in the decision to strip Haitians of their temporary protected status. FRANCE 24's Douglas Herbert tells us why this decision is a turning point for immigration in the US.

items.title: Supreme Court allows Trump to end protected status for Haitian and Syrian immigrants
items.standfirst: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.
items.content: The ruling opens the path for the Trump administration to deport hundreds of thousands of immigrants who have been living in the US for years.

clusters.title: Texas makes Bible passages required reading for millions of public school students
clusters.neutral_headline: Texas makes Bible passages required reading for millions of public school students
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Texas makes Bible passages required reading for millions of public school students
items.standfirst: Critics say mandating Bible reading for over 5m students breaches constitutional separation of church and stateThe Texas education board has approved a broad new statewide reading list that, for the first time, will make passages from the Bible required reading for more than 5 million public school students.Under the new initiative, Bible stories will become mandatory reading for millions of public school students in addition to a more standard collection of books, renewing debate over growing efforts in the US to increase the role of religion in classrooms. Continue reading...
items.content: Critics say mandating Bible reading for over 5m students breaches constitutional separation of church and stateThe Texas education board has approved a broad new statewide reading list that, for the first time, will make passages from the Bible required reading for more than 5 million public school students.Under the new initiative, Bible stories will become mandatory reading for millions of public school students in addition to a more standard collection of books, renewing debate over growing efforts in the US to increase the role of religion in classrooms. Continue reading...

items.title: Texas board approves adding Bible stories to required reading for public school students
items.standfirst: The reading list will take effect starting in 2030.
items.content: The reading list will take effect starting in 2030.
